author: dunkin-novice
goal: Stop /reading.html?mode=daily from rendering the daily card image twice on the result screen. The Phase 5 daily-result rebuild (commit 86fb5e8) added a ceremonial .panel--spread[data-spread-count='1'] inside renderDailyDetails — but startDailyReadingFlow's pre-existing .daily-motion-board (the deal-animation card stack) was being left in place after the reveal animation, so users saw both the revealed deal-animation card AND the new ceremonial spread card stacked vertically.
done: js/reading.js startDailyReadingFlow: added stageRefs.board.remove() immediately after stageRefs.deck.remove() (line ~2747). The deal animation has fully completed by this point (the await animateDailyReveal at line 2743 has resolved), so removing the board doesn't interrupt the reveal — it just clears the deal-stage card art so the ceremonial spread panel built by renderDailyDetails(cards, dict, stageRefs.stage) is the only card image on screen. 1 line of code + 4 lines of inline comment explaining why.
verified: md5 canonical == www == ios for js/reading.js
cp + cap sync ios completed; no plugin errors
Remote deploy: grep stageRefs.board.remove on https://meowtarot.com/js/reading.js returns the new call

renderQuestion (line 3367) and renderFull (line 3060) both start with readingContent.innerHTML = '' — neither has the two-stage-in-one-container pattern that daily mode had, so the fix is correctly scoped to daily only
notes: ROOT CAUSE: After the Phase 5 daily-result rebuild, two code paths were both emitting a card-art image into the same stageRefs.stage container: (a) createDailyMotionStage in startDailyReadingFlow built .daily-motion-board with buildCardArt(card, 'hero') for the deal animation; (b) renderDailyDetails(cards, dict, stageRefs.stage) appended a new .panel--spread with buildCardArt(card, 'thumb') for the ceremonial layout. The deal-animation board served its purpose by the time the reveal animation completed, so removing it is correct — it doesn't break the animation lifecycle. The 'duplicate card sector' was the visible symptom; the underlying issue was a layering assumption in the Phase 5 rebuild that the deal stage would be replaced (not augmented) when renderDailyDetails took over. ALTERNATIVE CONSIDERED: skip the Phase 5 .panel--spread emission inside renderDailyDetails when data-spread-count='1' is already set by the motion stage — rejected because the ceremonial spread carries the aura glow, archetype line, and lucky chip from the design doc that the deal-animation cards do NOT show. Keeping the ceremonial layout and removing the deal stage is the design-correct path.
filed: A future polish could add a fade-out animation on the .daily-motion-board before remove(), so the deal-animation card visually transitions into the ceremonial card position rather than disappearing abruptly. Currently the swap is instant — not jarring at typical Phase 5 sequence pacing, but a 200ms opacity fade would feel more deliberate. Out of scope here.

When auditing other reading modes during this fix: confirmed renderQuestion and renderFull both clear readingContent.innerHTML before rendering, so neither has the same pattern. If a future mode adds an animated pre-stage similar to the daily deal-animation, the same teardown discipline (remove pre-stage before appending result layout) should be applied.
affects: meowtarot.com/reading.html?mode=daily — daily reading result page now shows only one card image (the Phase 5 ceremonial spread panel) instead of two stacked cards. The deal animation sequence is unchanged; only the post-reveal teardown differs — the animation board is cleared right after the reveal so the ceremonial layout renders alone.
